draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer low visual noise and dark backgrounds for early morning/evening visibility and reduced cognitive load during focus on performance
facade.label: Dark and minimal
facade.acceptImplies: Probe darker interfaces, reduce color palette, test flat/minimal icon sets
facade.rejectImplies: Explore brighter, more colorful, detailed visual directions

facade.hypothesis: A conversational, encouraging tone with personality will resonate more than neutral instructional language for a runner-focused app
facade.label: Chatty coach
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers warm, motivational voice—lean into encouragement, casual language, personal connection in UI copy and notifications
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, straightforward, no-nonsense communication—optimize for clarity and efficiency over personality

evidence.hypothesis: Runners prefer low visual noise and dark backgrounds for early morning/evening visibility and reduced cognitive load during focus on performance
evidence.implication: Probe darker interfaces, reduce color palette, test flat/minimal icon sets

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar showing current conditions, with the main feed using card-based layout for individual runs/weather checks
facade.axisTargeted: navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs) + content grouping (cards vs list)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values always-visible context and modular, scannable content blocks
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers sequential, full-screen focus or continuous list scrolling over compartmentalized cards

facade.hypothesis: Runners want real-time heart rate or cadence visualized as a live animation overlay on their route, not a static readout.
facade.axisTargeted: Animated data overlay vs static metrics display
facade.label: Pace pulse on map
facade.acceptImplies: User values continuous motion feedback and wants kinetic data presentation throughout the experience.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers glanceable, settled information; animation feels noisy or distracting when checking vitals mid-run.

facade.hypothesis: Runners prioritize quick glance-ability and low visual friction over atmospheric immersion
facade.axisTargeted: digital vs organic; calm vs energetic
facade.label: Bright, minimal interface
facade.acceptImplies: Design favors speed and clarity—clean typography, high contrast, stripped-down data presentation. User wants to scan splits and alerts in 1 second while running.
facade.rejectImplies: Design should embrace warmth, personality, or organic texture—perhaps muted colors, illustrated elements, or a more meditative visual tone that feels less utilitarian.

facade.hypothesis: Runners want simultaneous weather context (where conditions change) and numeric precision (pace, temp, distance) without choosing between them
facade.axisTargeted: Information hierarchy — spatial separation vs integrated overlay
facade.label: Split screen: map + stats
facade.acceptImplies: Design should support parallel scanning of location-based and personal performance data; consider side-by-side or swipeable layouts
facade.rejectImplies: Design should prioritize either environmental awareness OR personal metrics, not force both; consider unified view or modal switching

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners want vibrant, high-contrast visuals to stay alert and energized during workouts, especially in daylight running conditions, contradicting the dark minimal preference
facade.label: Bright and colorful
facade.acceptImplies: Design should explore bright backgrounds, neon accents, multi-color status indicators for performance metrics
facade.rejectImplies: Dark minimal aesthetic is strongly preferred; bright designs create visual fatigue during active use

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
draft.html:
